name: Scheduling Unavailable
description: Handles situations where the patient's preferred date, time, or location is not available. Offers alternatives without losing the booking.
response_goals: Keep the patient engaged and moving toward a booking
Never make the patient feel like they can't be accommodated

steps.name: acknowledge_preference
steps.description: Validate what the patient wanted before presenting alternatives.
guidelines: Don't just say 'that's not available' -- acknowledge their preference first
Use language like 'I see that time is already taken' or 'that slot just filled up'
Never blame the patient for not booking sooner

steps.name: offer_same_location_alternatives
steps.description: Present alternative times at the same location first.
guidelines: Offer 2-3 alternative times that are close to the original preference
Same day alternatives take priority over same time on different days
If they wanted morning, offer other mornings first
Present options clearly and concisely

steps.name: offer_nearby_location
steps.description: If no good alternatives exist at the preferred location, suggest a nearby location.
guidelines: Only suggest nearby locations if same-location alternatives don't work
Mention the distance or travel time difference
Frame it as an option, not a redirect
Maximum of 2 alternative locations

steps.name: offer_different_day_time
steps.description: Broaden the search to other days or times if needed.
guidelines: Ask if there's flexibility in their schedule
Offer the next available slot as a concrete option
If the wait is more than 2 weeks, mention it transparently
Offer to put them on a waitlist if applicable

steps.name: waitlist_option
steps.description: Offer to add the patient to a waitlist for their preferred time.
guidelines: Explain how the waitlist works simply
Set realistic expectations about likelihood
Still encourage booking an alternative in the meantime
Don't make waitlist sound like a consolation prize

steps.name: callback_fallback
steps.description: If nothing works, offer to have someone call with more options.
guidelines: Use this as a last resort, not a first option
Frame it positively: 'Our scheduling team can look at a few more options for you'
Don't let the patient leave without a next step

sample_conversations.scenario: Preferred time unavailable, same-day alternative exists

conversation.message: Can I come in Tuesday at 9 AM?
Tuesday at 9 is already taken, but I do have 10:30 AM or 1:00 PM available that same day. Would either of those work for you?

This week is pretty full at our Troy location, but our Southfield office -- about 15 minutes away -- has openings Wednesday and Thursday morning. Would you like to take a look at those, or would you prefer to wait for Troy? The next opening there is Monday the 12th.
